social_quality.reason: Partner dancing and an open dance floor create lots of natural interaction beyond existing friend groups.

serendipity.reason: A bar/venue dance night can mix strangers, but the crowd is still likely self-selected and not broadly diverse.

uniqueness.reason: It’s explicitly a recurring “2nd Tuesday” event rather than a one-off.

community_building.reason: Regular dance nights tend to form a repeat crowd and scene, but it’s lighter-weight than a club/volunteer org.

solo_friendliness.reason: They explicitly welcome people without partners and the shared activity makes it easy to join in.

value_for_price.reason: A $5 cover for a full dance night is typically a strong value.

events.name: Joe's Cousin One Man Band
events.description: Wyatt - AKA Joe's Cousin One Man Band is the One Man Rockabilly Honkytonk Show. No band members, no roadies, just one man. Performing at Roadhouses, Bars, Honkytonks, Music Halls, and other venues and recording raw, original music, while putting his own spin on the coolest covers, this talented One Man Band gains fans and friends everywhere he goes. His band is named after his cousin Joe, a cowboy in Texas who rides a horse named HellBitch. https://facebook.com/joescousinmusic/ https://www.instagram.com/joescousinonemanband https://www.joescousinmusic.com/ https://open.spotify.com/artist/0JLB0

social_quality.reason: As a bar concert, most interaction will be within existing friend groups with limited structured mixing across strangers.

serendipity.reason: A niche genre show in an indoor venue tends to draw a fairly self-selected crowd, though bars can still produce some chance encounters.

uniqueness.reason: A specific one-night performance is time-bound, but the act and format are not inherently rare and likely recur at different venues.

community_building.reason: Attending supports a local venue and artist but doesn’t inherently create ongoing membership or durable civic ties.

solo_friendliness.reason: Showing up alone is normal at a bar show, but there’s little built-in structure that makes it easy to meet new people.

value_for_price.reason: If priced like a typical small-venue bar show (often free to modest cover), the entertainment-to-cost ratio is usually solid.

events.description: Participants will write movement-focused poetry and plan exploratory parkour moves using a worksheet developed by Gabe Life 100. Then, the group will move outside and try their moves together. Finally, the group will come together for a short reflection and debrief. All approaches to movement will be celebrated. events.name: LSAF – Interactive Arts Tent – Instituto Gráphico de Chicago
events.description: This project is a community invitation to help create a piece of public art. The Project – Janet Austin and Instituto Gráfico de Chicago (IGC) are creating a public sculpture to be permanently installed at the Logan Square Blue Line CTA station in Chicago. The Big Idea – Instead of just making the sculpture by themselves in a studio, the artists want the artwork to reflect the actual people of Chicago. events.name: Comfort Music : Elijah McLaughlin + Tommaso Moretti
events.description: Chicago based musicians/bandleaders Elijah McLaughlin and Tommaso Moretti join forces in a new project that delves deep into the extended worlds of improvised music. Blending minimalism, jazz, ambient psychedelia, and experimental rock to create a sound that is full of melodic lyricism, mesmerizing grooves, and shifting harmonic textures. The music is rooted in the moment, and is sure to take the listener on a expansive sonic journey.

events.name: Comfort Film Presents Fais le mort! (Play Dead!) 2023
events.description: Comfort Film Presents Fais le mort! (Play Dead!) 2023 Matthew Lancit grew up in Canada, where teenagers of his generation were marked by the “body horror” film genre. Taking cue from that, Play Dead! is culled from his diary of his family’s quotidian, filmed over five years. He stages the ‘diabetes’ as a hidden, alien presence that haunts his apartment and the very depths of his body. Using performative and burlesque codes, form and content feed off each other as the filmmaker’s body transforms gradually into a haunted shell. Laced with comedy, irony and tenderness, Play Dead! mediates the com

events.name: July Adult Book Discussion: True Biz by Sara Novic
events.venue: Bucktown-Wicker Park Branch, Chicago Public Library
events.description: Join us in July in honor of Disability Pride Month to discuss True Biz , a novel by Sara Novic. This is a story of sign language and lip-reading, disability and civil rights, isolation and injustice, first love and loss, and, above all, great persistence, daring, and joy. Absorbing and assured, idiosyncratic and relatable, this is an unforgettable journey into the Deaf community and a universal celebration of human connection. Fresh food is tightly controlled, and the divide between the wealthy and everyone else

events.name: Good Day
events.description: Presented by A Short Leap Theatre Company Recommended for ages 14+ A prodigal daughter returns home, only she can’t get any farther than the front lawn. When three seemingly inconsequential strangers arrive for house calls, she’s pulled and pushed towards the front door and a life-changing confession. Under the auspices of California sunshine and a suburban wasps nest, an average “good day” is revealed to be made-up of so much more in this meditation on grief and reconciliation. Directed by Nathan Dale Short* Assistant Director, Vanessa Copeland* Featuring Sydney Feldman, Rachel Herriges*, Eri

events.name: SOLD OUT - Ben Palmer
events.description: Presented by The Den Theatre This event has a 2 item minimum. Ben Palmer is a comedian known for his distinctive blend of satire, trolling, and social commentary. His bits often involve elaborate pranks that blur the line between reality and parody. In one of his most well-known stunts, Palmer spent months pretending to work for a social media company while communicating with former U.S. Congressman Ted Yoho, recording conversations that eventually led to Yoho admitting on tape that he had called Congresswoman Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez a “f---ing b----” despite previously telling Congress the i

events.name: Rollin' Rec at Walsh
events.description: The Rollin' Recreation activity van offers fun for the whole family at a number of parks throughout the city. The vans drive in, and the fun rolls out - sports, games, and fitness activities. Come join us and enjoy these activities with others in your community. No registration required, just come out and have fun.

events.name: Emily Kuhn Quintet at Wicker
events.description: Emily Kuhn’s quintet features stalwart Chicago improvisers: Kuhn - trumpet/flugelhorn, Meghan Stagl - piano/voice, Erik Skov - guitar, Kitt Lyles - bass, Gustavo Cortiñas - drums. Since releasing their debut album, “Ghosts of Us” (2023), they’ve performed at prestigious venues including Champaign Urbana Jazz Festival, Stanford Jazz Festival, Hey Nonny’s Women’s Jazz Festival, and Festival of New Trumpet Music. They’re celebrating the release of their new album “Feathered Things,” an exploration of hope and resilience through images of snow, fire, and flight. www.emilykuhntrumpet.com

events.name: RiverLab: River Exploration Paddle at River
events.description: Discover the Chicago River and all the critters who call it home on this beginner friendly guided canoe paddle. Be sure to wear comfortable clothing and remember to bring water and sunscreen. All equipment is included in your registration fee. Basic instruction will be provided, beginners are encouraged to join! Please plan to arrive 15 minutes before the program start time. Youth under 18 must be accompanied by an adult. The Chicago Park District's RiverLab program is a series of educational events and activities dedicated to reconnecting residents of Chicago with their river.

events.name: Toddlers, Tunes, & Turtles at Welles
events.description: Toddlers, Tunes and Turtles invites our youngest explorers to visit your park and explore! This outdoor nature program encourages families to enjoy the park together. Join us for live animal interactions, nature scavenger hunts, and music performances from Old Town School of Folk Music.

events.name: Water Safety Training at Montrose Beach
events.description: Join us for free safety orientations at your Chicago beach! Learn how to keep yourself and others safe at the beach, recognize a swimmer in distress, and how to respond in an emergency. Provides basic water safety training that will help all Chicagoans enjoy a safe summer at the beach or pool. Training is FREE! Registration optional.

events.name: Movies in the Parks at Mozart
events.description: ZOOTOPIA 2 Judy Hopps and Nick Wilde pursue a pit viper named Gary De'Snake across Zootopia and try to clear their names after being framed. PG | 108 minutes | 2025 *Closed Captioned Start time is approximate, all movies begin at dusk.

events.name: RiverLab: Thursday Evening Paddle Club at River
events.description: Looking for a fun way to spend your Thursday evenings? Join our Thursday Evening Paddle Club for a relaxed and social paddle perfect for connecting with other nature lovers and paddling enthusiasts. Whether you’re a seasoned paddler or just starting out, all are welcome! Be sure to wear comfortable clothing and remember to bring water and sunscreen. All equipment is included in your registration fee. Basic instruction will be provided, and beginners are encouraged to join! Please plan to arrive 15 minutes before the program start time. Youth under 18 must be accompanied by an adult. The Chicag

events.name: Shakespeare Blood Oath: Henry VIII at Maplewood
events.description: Shakespeare Blood Oath has vowed to perform every one of Shakespeare’s plays, in an order we determined by pulling them all out of a hat. Well, it’s finally time to do a bad one, one even Shakespeare wasn’t proud of — Henry VIII. Frankly, we couldn’t be more excited! This year, SBO is injecting the chaos and energy of short-form improv into Shakespeare, with games, audience participation, and more. The biggest twist … the SBO players will rehearse to perform every role, and each performance will be cast in front of the audience at the beginning of every show! https://www.shakespearebloodoath.c

events.name: CuriosaMente at Avondale
events.description: Featuring circus, puppetry and live music, CuriosaMente presents El Show de Las Preguntas (The Show of Questions)!! Inspired by the illustrations Paloma Valdivia gave Pablo Neruda’s Book of Questions/Libro de Las Preguntas, a Cat sets out to find answers to their many questions such as: ¿Dónde termina el arco iris, en tu alma o en el horizonte? Where does the rainbow end, in your soul or on the horizon? Family Friendly and bilingual songs in Spanish and English.

events.name: Eliy Orcko, Mohawk Johnson, The Beloved Davida
events.description: Eliy Orcko is a jazz rap artist blending lo-fi and jazz-hop, inspired by Southern Florida's serene landscapes. His music, rich with introspective lyrics on life, love, and the human condition, offers a nostalgic, introspective journey. Known for his laid-back flow and melodic del

events.name: LSAF – Workshop – Millstone Presents: The Last Word is Not a Word
events.description: The Last Word is Not a Word explores the possibilities of how to write towards endings that open up a poem rather than close it. The real purpose of that common writing advice to "show, don't tell" is that the meaning writers cast their nets for is often found most fully in what is not said, or even at times in what is unable to be said. As Ludwig Wittengenstein wrote, "There is indeed the inexpressible. This shows itself." The workshop will feature a discussion of readings by Eve Ewing and Tracie Morris followed by a writing prompt. Millstone Presents: The Last Word is Not a Word Sunday, June

events.name: LSAF – Performances – The Mill, a variety show and open mic with Millstone
events.description: Please join us for The Mill (@millstone.mag) , a variety show and open mic that raises money for local Chicago charities. The night will begin with featured performers, including a stilts walker and folk musician. After the featured performances, is an all-genres open mic – all art mediums and styles are welcome – comedians, clowns, writers, puppeteers, musicians, and whatever in-between. For this show, The Mill is fundraising for Chicago Books for Women in Prison. The Mill, a Variety Show and Open Mic Millstone Friday | June 26 – 28, 2026 7 – 9 p interior of Comfort Station Millstone curates

events.name: Quiet Comfort: Erez Dessel / Beth McDonald
events.description: Erez and Beth have played and toured extensively with Ken Vandermark's Edition Redux for the past 3 years, and are excited to collaborate in a rare duo set. Their performance for Quiet Comfort will explore the idea of what it means create "quiet and quietude" even with amplified sound. Erez Dessel is a pianist, composer, and curator living and working in Chicago, Illinois. Dessel’s work explores the piano as a vehicle for human connection. A primary part of his practice is the history of the instrument as a tool for storytelling and world-building. Beth McDonald is a classically trained tuba p

events.name: Comfort Music: PXM & Kimmel/Langdon Duo
events.description: Jack Langdon (organ and ojibwe wooden flute) and Jeff Kimmel (clarinets) play quiet, abstract music which integrates elements of the American experimental and improvised music traditions into inscrutable forms. Their album "the other side of the air" was released by Dinzu Artefacts in 2024, described by Peter Margasak: "Sounds align, unleashing the infectious sweep of beating patterns, and clear intonation becomes sour with the incursion of the slightest pitch adjustment." events.name: Wendy Eisenberg and More Eaze / William Covert and Bruce Lamont
events.description: Doors: 8PM / Show: 9PM / 21+ Wendy Eisenberg Wendy Eisenberg has spent the past decade as a fixture of independent music and an artist of inspired multiplicity. As a singer-songwriter, improviser, and virtuoso guitarist, the coordinates of their artistry are ever-shifting, from art-rock to jazz to blistering free improv and eloquent folk. On catalog highlights including 2020s Auto and the 2024 free-jazz sprawler Viewfinder, theyve made a signature of ambition. events.name: Midwife / Amulets / Desert Liminal
events.description: Doors: 8PM / Show: 9PM / 21+ Madeline Johnston is a self taught multi-instrumentalist and recording engineer, currently based in Southern Colorado by way of Denver, where she spent the last decade developing her sound and focusing on her artistic community. Midwife began while Madeline was a resident of the beloved DIY space Rhinoceropolis in 2014. On stage, Johnston is known for her sparse, emotionally charged experimental pop music that can silence a room. But then there is something undeniably different about Thee Marloes and their music, something new and distinct. And while you may be acquainted with soul music, youve probably never heard it from Surabaya, Indonesiathe place Thee Marloes call home. events.description: Doors: 8PM / Show: 9PM / 21+Six years after their last record, Philadelphias DARK THOUGHTS have reemerged as dialed-in as ever on this brand new slab. The tunes are tight and concise, never overstaying their welcome and always getting the point across within their brief duration. Applying the poppy and speedy formula of classic SCREECHING WEASEL and RAMONES to maximum effect, the songs hit hard with relatability, realism, and hope. events.description: Doors: 8PM / Show: 9PM / 21+ Julie Doiron is back with I Thought Of You, her first solo record since 2012s So Many Days. events.description: Doors: 8PM / Show: 9PM / 21+C.O.L.A. is sort of a self-titled album. Its an acronym for Cost of Living Adjustment, a fitting conceptual framework for the bands third record. Why? Because C.O.L.A. considers, among other things, socialism vs. hell. It considers: rolling the dice of life. The eerie and sweet pangs that nostalgia can provoke.
